name: Stickynote Workflow
description: Automated workflow: Stickynote Workflow. This workflow integrates 10 different services: stickyNote, formTrigger, youTube, agent, outputParserStructured. It contains 13 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

nodes.name: Youtube Meta Generator

parameters.text:
=You are an AI content generator specialized in crafting high-converting YouTube metadata for videos related to stores, shops, memberships, and business promotions. Your task is to generate a **structured JSON output** optimized for YouTube SEO and audience engagement based on the provided video transcript and focus keywords. Use "syncbricks information" tool to collect relevant social media, courses, website and affilite links and ensure to add the relavent links in description. Major course or affilite link should be used as hook in the beginning of the description.

### **Output Requirements:**
1. **Title:** A compelling, SEO-friendly title optimized for search and audience interest.
2. **Description:** A detailed, keyword-rich summary of the video, incorporating relevant keywords naturally and including a clear value proposition.
3. **Keywords:** Single line of all possible keywords with  at least 450 characters in total with comma in between each keyword relevant to the video content to enhance discoverability.
4. **Hashtags:** Single line of 5 to 10 relevant hashtags, without the comma that align with the video's theme.
5. **Affiliate Links:** Contextually relevant affiliate links that match the video content. Only provide link don't add unnessary boxes.
7. **Call to Action (CTA):** A persuasive CTA encouraging viewers to **subscribe, like, share, visit a store, or sign up for membership**.
8. **Additional Promotional Links:** Gather and include relevant **course links, business website links, or related references** that add value to the audience.
9. **Channel Hashtags:** Append **#EnterpriseIT #BusinessIntelligence #TechSolutions #ITInsights #HomeLab #Gadgets #TechReview #ITTips #SyncBricks #AmjidAli** at the end of the description.

### **Instructions:**
- Ensure that **affiliate links are directly related** to the video topic.
- Use **natural language and avoid keyword stuffing** to maintain a user-friendly tone.
- Don't add social media profiles, and syncbricks websit link, only add the affilaite and promotion links
- The description should be **at least 150 words properly formatted with lines and paragraphs** for better YouTube SEO.
 - Avoid adding [] brackets
- Structure the output in a **well-formatted JSON format** for automation.

##Example of Affialite and promotion Links ##
n8n : {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}
Full Course : {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or udemy link


### **Here is the existing Video Details:**
- **Transcript:** {{ $json['Video Transcript'] }}
- **Focus Keywords:** {{ $json['Focus Keywords'] }}

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: No custom credentials required for utility-only operations.
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "On form submission".
Open the "Sticky Note11" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "syncbricks information" node (n8n-nodes-base.googleDocsTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Youtube Meta Generator" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "YouTube" node (n8n-nodes-base.youTube) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Output Parser" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Form" node (n8n-nodes-base.form) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Extract Video ID".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Format Tags".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
